name: Devil Fruit: Mera Mera no Mi (Example)

description.value:
Archetype Artifact — Devil Fruit (Logia)

A bright orange, flame-shaped fruit. Consuming it grants the Flame-Human archetype, transforming the user into a living embodiment of fire.

Passive (Logia Intangibility): Non-magical physical attacks pass harmlessly through your flame-form. You gain immunity to fire damage and weakness 5 to water damage. This passive is suppressed while Sea-Bound.

Activate [one-action] (concentrate, fire) — You hurl a bolt of fire: ranged Strike, 60 feet, 2d6 fire damage (add your level to damage). Targets may attempt a DC (8 + your level + highest mental modifier) basic Reflex save.

Trait: Sea-Bound. While submerged in standing seawater (at least waist-deep) or in contact with Seastone, you are Slowed 1, Clumsy 1, and cannot use any actions or passives granted by your Devil Fruit archetype. If fully submerged, you are Stunned until removed from the water.

A creature may only ever consume one Devil Fruit. Consuming a second is instantly fatal.
description.gm: Logia intangibility is one of the most powerful effects in the game. The suppression via devil-fruit:suppressed roll option is critical. Expand with your campaign's feat chain. The physical immunity RE is listed below but you should confirm the predicate logic suits your system version.
